title: Implementing Breadth-First Search
scenario: Write a Python function that performs a breadth-first search (BFS) traversal on an unweighted graph. The function should accept an adjacency list representation of the graph and a target node as input and return a list of nodes in the order they were visited. The output should be a code snippet, formatted according to standard Python conventions.
constraints: Must handle edge cases for disconnected graphs

evaluationCriteria.description: Verify that the Python code snippet is syntactically correct, following standard Python conventions.
Check whether the generated code meets the specified constraints (queue-based approach and edge case handling).
Evaluate the correctness of the generated code, ensuring it accurately performs a BFS traversal on the input graph.
Assess the code's readability and maintainability, considering comment quality, variable naming, and overall structure.
